summary: The core note-enrichment slice is mostly in place and the reported gates passed, but I would not promote conservatively yet. The required design contract says note enrichment must fail visibly even when the AI call fails or times out; the current implementation issues a bare `fetch` in `src/features/ai/client.ts` with no timeout/abort path, so a hung Mina request can leave a note stuck in `pending` indefinitely with no visible failed state or retry. That means exit criterion 2 is not satisfied in substance for timeout-class failures, even though create/update triggering, metadata surfacing, and the reported `verify`/`@ai-real` success paths look correct.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Saving a note triggers enrichment automatically on both create and update via `src/app/app/notes/actions.ts`.
Generated note summary/tags are surfaced in the note editor and owner note-related surfaces, and public published-note surfaces also render generated metadata.
The provided deterministic check summary shows `npm run verify` passed.
The provided deterministic check summary shows `npm run test:e2e -- --grep @ai-real` passed against the configured real endpoint.
missing_requirements: Exit criterion 2 is not satisfied in substance: timeout-class AI failures do not reliably transition to a visible failed state with retry.

The required design doc `docs/design-docs/ai-enrichment-lifecycle.md` says enrichment must remain reliable when the AI call fails or times out, but the current client has no timeout handling to enforce that behavior.
There is no automated proof of the configured-endpoint timeout/failure path; the failure E2E in `tests/e2e/home.spec.ts` is skipped when real AI env vars are present.
human_review_triggers: `src/features/ai/client.ts` performs `fetch(request.endpoint, request.init)` without a timeout/abort mechanism, so a hung endpoint can strand notes in `pending` indefinitely.
The evaluator gate passed on success-path evidence, but the visible failed-state contract under timeout conditions was not demonstrated end-to-end.
